h1: Mobile Document Capture Workflow for Field Teams

hero.headline: Streamline Field Document Processing with Mobile Capture
hero.subheadline: Set up efficient mobile document capture workflow for field teams to instantly convert photos and scans to structured Excel data

summary: A comprehensive workflow guide for field teams to capture documents using mobile devices and automatically extract data to Excel spreadsheets. Covers setup, capture techniques, processing automation, and quality control for consistent results in field operations.
whoItsFor: Field service technicians handling invoices and work orders
Sales representatives processing contracts and purchase orders
whenThisIsRelevant: Teams spend hours manually entering data from field documents
Document quality varies significantly in field conditions
Need immediate access to structured data from mobile-captured documents
supportedInputs: Photos of documents taken with mobile devices
Scanned PDF files from portable scanners
Digital documents received via mobile apps
expectedOutputs: Excel spreadsheets with extracted field data
commonChallenges: Inconsistent photo quality affecting data accuracy
Managing multiple document types with different field requirements
Ensuring secure document handling in field environments
Coordinating between mobile capture and back-office processing
howItWorksSteps: Set up mobile capture guidelines for consistent photo quality and lighting
Configure custom field extraction templates for each document type your team handles
Establish batch processing schedules to handle documents captured throughout the day
Implement quality control checks to verify extracted data accuracy before final processing
whyPdfExcelAiFits: AI-powered extraction works with mobile photos achieving 99%+ accuracy on clear documents
Batch processing handles multiple documents captured during field visits efficiently
Custom field selection adapts to different document types field teams encounter
Secure processing with automatic file deletion protects sensitive field documentation
limitations: Accuracy depends on photo quality - poor lighting or blurry images may require retakes
Handwritten notes on field documents have limited recognition compared to printed text
Very complex multi-page documents may need manual review for complete accuracy

faq.question: What mobile photo quality is needed for accurate data extraction?
faq.answer: Clear, well-lit photos with readable text work best. Avoid shadows, ensure the document fills most of the frame, and check that all text is sharp and legible before processing.

faq.question: Can the workflow handle different document types in one batch?
faq.answer: Yes, you can process mixed document types together. The AI identifies document types automatically, though you may want separate workflows for documents requiring different field extraction templates.

faq.question: How quickly can field teams get extracted data back?
faq.answer: Processing typically completes within minutes for small batches. Larger batches or complex documents may take longer, but you'll receive structured Excel files as soon as processing finishes.

faq.question: What happens if mobile-captured documents have poor quality?
faq.answer: The system will extract what it can identify clearly. Documents with poor lighting, blur, or other quality issues may have missing fields or lower accuracy, requiring manual review of the output.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
OCR technology handles both digital and scanned document formats
